commit_message:
[rtl] Harden lockstep enable against FI [rtl] Harden lockstep enable against FI Currently, the dual-core lockstep FI mitigation is enabled/disabled using a single bit. For transient bit-flips, this is not problematic, as one bit-flip into this signal and one bit into the Ibex is required to threaten the security of the system.

However, a permanent stuck-at-0 fault could disable the lockstep completely by targeting this signal. Then, only a single, additional fault (transient or permanent) is

examples.patch:
@@ -46,7 +46,11 @@ module ibex_core import ibex_pkg::*; #(
 ) (
   // Clock and Reset
   input  logic                         clk_i,
+  // Internally generated resets in ibex_lockstep cause IMPERFECTSCH warnings.
+  // TODO: Remove when upgrading Verilator #2134.
+  /* verilator lint_off IMPERFECTSCH */
   input  logic                         rst_ni,
+  /* verilator lint_on IMPERFECTSCH */
 
   input  logic [31:0]                  hart_id_i,
   input  logic [31:0]                  boot_addr_i,
